message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should apply 30% surcharge for sword with enchantment exactly 5 (0-year customer) — 100 base + 30 enchantment + 10 first insurance = 140 + 5 fee = 145"
Expected behavior: Items with enchantment >= 5 get a 30% surcharge on their base premium (item-specific modifier)
Current error: Expected 145, received 115
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

Current calculateItemSurcharges only handles cursed. Add enchantment >= 5 check.

The function currently:
```typescript
function calculateItemSurcharges(items: any[]): number {
  return items
    .filter((item: any) => item.cursed)
    .reduce((total: number, item: any) => total + (BASE_PREMIUM[item.type] ?? 0) * CURSED_SURCHARGE_RATE, 0);
}
```

This only handles cursed. We need to also check enchantment >= 5 for each item. The surcharges are additive per item - each item can have both cursed AND enchantment surcharges.

Minimal approach: change calculateItemSurcharges to iterate over all items and sum both surcharges where applicable.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll start by reading the current implementation and test files to understand the exact state of things.

content.text: Now let me run the tests to confirm the current failure.

input.description: Run tests to see current failure

content.text:
The test expects 145 but receives 115. The difference is exactly 30, which is the 30% surcharge on the 100 base premium for a sword with enchantment >= 5. The current `calculateItemSurcharges` only handles cursed items. I need to add the enchantment >= 5 surcharge.

The minimal change: add a constant for the enchantment surcharge rate and modify `calculateItemSurcharges` to also sum enchantment surcharges for items with enchantment >= 5.

content.text:
All 15 tests pass (including the previously failing enchantment surcharge test), with 26 remaining as todo.

**Green Phase Complete:**

**Implementation**: Added enchantment surcharge logic to `calculateItemSurcharges` in `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-21_15-29-15_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-6-portkey-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts`. Introduced two constants (`ENCHANTMENT_SURCHARGE_RATE = 0.3` and `ENCHANTMENT_THRESHOLD = 5`) and rewrote the function to iterate over all items, applying the cursed surcharge when `item.cursed` is true and the enchantment surcharge when `item.enchantment >= 5`. Both surcharges are additive per item.

**Result**: All tests now pass (15 passing, 26 todo).

**Approach**: This is minimal because it only adds what the current test demands -- a 30% surcharge on base premium for items with enchantment >= 5. The function was restructured from a filter/reduce chain to a simple loop so it can check both conditions per item, which is the simplest way to handle the two independent surcharge conditions. No future features were added.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
